description: Caddy Aegis framework: security audit framework (10 slash commands + 4 external CLI deps; semgrep WSL-only)

script: if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw 'git clone failed. Ensure you are added as a collaborator to meetcaddy/aegis and your git CLI is authenticated (run: gh auth login).' }
if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Pop-Location; throw 'git checkout v0.1.0 SHA failed.' }

notes: Aegis framework files installed to: $(scoop prefix caddy-aegis)\share\caddy-aegis\
External CLI dependencies auto-installed via Scoop (main bucket):
gitleaks, grype, syft, trivy
IMPORTANT: semgrep is NOT auto-installed on Windows.
semgrep upstream has no native Windows support (recommends WSL).
Aegis runs gracefully without semgrep; semgrep-based checks are skipped.
For full coverage on Windows, customers can install semgrep via WSL:
wsl --install
wsl pip install semgrep
Then invoke semgrep from WSL when running /aegis:audit. Advanced setup;
not required for v1.0 customer onboarding.
To activate in %USERPROFILE%\.claude\, run the caddy-link helper:
caddy-link
Commands installed (type /aegis: in Claude Code for the full menu):
/aegis:init        initialize AEGIS in a target project
/aegis:audit       run a full or targeted diagnostic audit
/aegis:report      view the audit report
/aegis:status      show current audit state and progress
/aegis:resume      resume an interrupted audit
/aegis:validate    test tool install and framework integrity
/aegis:playbook    generate a remediation playbook
/aegis:transform   run the full Transform pipeline post-audit
/aegis:remediate   generate remediation plans for findings
/aegis:guardrails  generate AI assistant rules from findings
PREREQUISITE: requires GitHub collaborator access to meetcaddy/aegis
(provisioned during Caddy onboarding) AND an authenticated git CLI
(`gh auth login` recommended).
